MONEYBAGG YO

The Game · The Grind · The Glory

The dancer on this floor reps Moneybagg Yo — born DeMario DeWayne White Jr. on September 22, 1991, and raised in the Walker Homes area of South Memphis, Tennessee. He came up loving hip-hop and drew early inspiration from Southern voices like Boosie, Yo Gotti, Juvenile and Future, chasing rap as a way out of the trenches.

  • Real NameDeMario D. White Jr.
  • FromSouth Memphis, TN · 901
  • GenreMelodic Trap / Southern Hip-Hop
  • Active2011 – present
  • LabelCMG / Interscope / Bread Gang

The Grind

He started stacking mixtapes around 2011–2012, building a real buzz across Memphis and the South. Taking the Mixtape of the Year honor at the 2016 Memphis Hip Hop Awards marked a turning point, and later that year he signed with Yo Gotti’s Collective Music Group in a joint venture with Interscope.

The Glory

Commercial tapes Federal 3X (2017) and 2 Heartless (2018) broke him into the mainstream, landing in the Billboard 200’s top 20. A run of studio albums followed — Reset, 43va Heartless and Time Served — before A Gangsta’s Pain (2021) hit No. 1 on the all-genre chart and delivered the hit “Wockesha,” a top-20 single built on his signature booming 808s and real-life-pain honesty.
